question: How does tokenization help an NLP model read text?
answer: Tokenization is the process of splitting text into smaller units such as words subwords or characters so a model can process the sequence. [2] Word tokenization keeps whole words when possible while subword tokenization breaks rare or complex words into smaller learned pieces. [3]

question: Why are embeddings usually more useful than one hot vectors for meaning?

question: Why do neural networks need nonlinear activations instead of only linear layers?

question: Why might a practitioner choose a GRU when they want a simpler recurrent unit?

question: How does self attention let a token use information from the rest of the sentence?
